alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DGM-based channel estimation technique outperforms conventional channel estimation techniques in practical ranges of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DGM-based channel estimation technique outperforms conventional channel estimation techniques in practical ranges of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim directly, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique achieves near-optimal performance using only a few downlink pilot measurements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique achieves near-optimal performance using only a few downlink pilot measurements.
alignments.rationale: This claim is preserved verbatim in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method estimates frequency-independent underlying channel parameters via uplink training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method estimates frequency-independent underlying channel parameters via uplink training by exploiting the partial reciprocity of uplink and downlink channels.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the mechanism (exploiting partial reciprocity) used for uplink-based estimation, clarifying how the estimation is achieved while keeping the claim's scope and confidence similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The frequency-specific underlying channel parameters are estimated via downlink training using a very short training signal.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not reproduce the claim about frequency-specific parameter estimation via very short downlink training and instead emphasizes incorporating priors, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of factorized flow maps as mid-level representations improves the bridging of perception and control modules in robotic frameworks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating factorized flow maps as mid-level representations improves the bridging of perception and control modules in robotic frameworks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution directly with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The configurable framework developed allows for the analysis of the impacts of factorized optical flow maps on deep reinforcement learning agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The configurable framework developed allows for the analysis of the impacts of factorized optical flow maps on deep reinforcement learning agents.
alignments.rationale: The claim about the configurable framework is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the advantages of factorized flow maps in various scenarios involving static and dynamic objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the advantages of factorized flow maps in various scenarios involving static and dynamic objects.
alignments.rationale: Experimental findings are repeated without substantive change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation of flow factorization in real-world scenarios supports the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation of flow factorization in real-world scenarios supports the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same concluding validation claim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Having a maximum EA burden greater than 75% when untreated had a 22% increased chance of a poor outcome (severe disability or death).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with a maximum epileptiform activity burden of 75% or more when untreated had a mean 22.27% increased chance of a poor outcome compared to those with a maximum activity of 0 to less than 25%.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with a more precise percentage and an explicit comparator group, adding detail but not changing the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mild but long-lasting EA increased the risk of a poor outcome by 14%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Moderate but long-lasting epileptiform activity (mean burden 2% to <10%) increased the risk of a poor outcome by mean 13.52%.
alignments.rationale: The effect size is essentially the same but the publication refines the burden category and gives a more precise estimate, clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effect sizes were heterogeneous depending on pre-admission profile, with patients with hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy (HIE) or acquired brain injury (ABI) being more affected.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effect sizes of epileptiform activity on outcomes were heterogeneous depending on preadmission profiles, with patients having hypoxic-ischaemic encephalopathy or acquired brain injury being more adversely affected.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same heterogeneity observation and explicitly the same subgroups; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interventions should put a higher priority on patients with an average EA burden higher than 10%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interventions should prioritize patients with an average epileptiform activity burden of 10% or greater, and treatment should be more conservative when maximum activity burden is low.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same prioritization threshold (10%) and pairs it with the related conservative-treatment recommendation already present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Treatment should be more conservative when the maximum EA burden is low.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interventions should prioritize patients with an average epileptiform activity burden of 10% or greater, and treatment should be more conservative when maximum activity burden is low.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same conservative-treatment recommendation in the same combined conclusion as the prioritization statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Treatment should be tailored to individual preadmission profiles as the potential for epileptiform activity to cause harm depends on age, medical history, and reason for admission.
alignments.rationale: This treatment-tailoring recommendation appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it is an added, more detailed clinical recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The WaveDiff model represents a performance breakthrough compared to the existing state-of-the-art data-driven approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The WaveDiff model represents a performance breakthrough compared to existing state-of-the-art data-driven approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim about WaveDiff representing a performance breakthrough with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pixel reconstruction errors decrease 6-fold at observation resolution and 44-fold for a 3x super-resolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pixel reconstruction errors decrease six-fold at observation resolution and 44-fold for a 3x super-resolution.
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance claims are carried over verbatim into the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ellipticity errors are reduced at least 20 times, and the size error is reduced more than 250 times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ellipticity errors are reduced at least 20 times, and the size error is reduced more than 250 times.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same quantitative error-reduction claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for the capture of PSF chromatic variations due to diffraction using only noisy broad-band in-focus observations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework successfully captures the PSF chromatic variations due to diffraction using only noisy broad-band in-focus observations.
alignments.rationale: The core claim is unchanged, but the publication presents the result with stronger wording/certainty (from medium to high), indicating amplified confidence in the outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed distance, linearFGW, effectively measures the similarity between graphs by considering both node features and graph structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed distance, LinearFGW, effectively measures the dissimilarity between graphs by considering node features and structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological contribution (measurement of graph similarity/dissimilarity via features and structure); wording difference (similarity vs dissimilarity) is nominal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LinearFGW is significantly faster for computing kernel matrices compared to pairwise OT-based distances, especially for large-scale data sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LinearFGW is more efficient for computing a kernel matrix than pairwise OT-based distances, particularly fused Gromov-Wasserstein.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the comparison by specifically naming fused Gromov-Wasserstein and frames the claim as efficiency for kernel-matrix computation, clarifying the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical properties of linearFGW have been discussed, supporting its application in graph similarity measurement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical analysis and experimental results indicate that LinearFGW improves performance compared to existing state-of-the-art graph distances in graph classification and clustering tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication goes beyond describing theoretical properties to assert empirical improvement against state-of-the-art, broadening and strengthening the original theoretical-only claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of linearFGW in classification and clustering tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical analysis and experimental results indicate that LinearFGW improves performance compared to existing state-of-the-art graph distances in graph classification and clustering tasks.
alignments.rationale: The experimental-effectiveness claim maps directly to the publication's combined theory+experiment claim, but the publication assigns a more moderate certainty (medium) than the preprint's higher stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cross-evaluation model highlights a broader range of realistic use-cases for assessing ML-NIDS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cross-evaluation model highlights a broader range of realistic use-cases for assessing ML-NIDS.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution statement with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of existing labelled data for cross-evaluations can extend the detection surface of ML-NIDS without additional labelling costs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of existing labeled data for cross-evaluating ML-NIDS can extend their detection surface without additional labeling costs.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about leveraging existing labeled data to extend detection surface, with only minor spelling differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The XeNIDS framework enables reliable cross-evaluations based on Network Flows.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework XeNIDS enables reliable cross-evaluations based on Network Flows.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same framework capability without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cross-evaluations of ML-NIDS reveal both concealed potential and risks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using XeNIDS on six well-known datasets reveals both the potential and risks associated with cross-evaluations of ML-NIDS.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the empirical basis (six datasets) for the finding and reports it with higher certainty, clarifying and amplifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Domain corpora have opposite effects on traditional word embedding models for text classification and named entity recognition tasks but can further improve the performance of BERT-based models in all tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Domain corpora can further improve the performance of static word embedding-based DL models and contextual word embedding-based DL models in text classification and named entity recognition tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication contradicts the preprint's statement of "opposite effects" on traditional embeddings by asserting improvement for both static and contextual models, changing the claim's direction and broadening its applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BERT-based models dramatically outperform traditional methods in all IR tasks, with maximum improvements of 5.4% and 10.1% in the F1 score, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contextual word embedding-based DL models significantly outperform the static word embedding-based DL methods in text classification and named entity recognition tasks, with maximum improvements of 8.1% and 3.8% in the F1 score, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the superiority claim but changes the framing (contextual vs static) and reports different maximum improvement magnitudes, revising the quantitative details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research demonstrates the advantages of domain corpora and pretrained DL models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research demonstrates the advantages of domain corpora and pretrained DL models in the AEC domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion, explicitly situating it in the AEC domain but not changing the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work opens the first domain-specific dataset and pretrained language model for the AEC domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work opens the first domain-specific dataset and pretrained language models named ARCBERT for the AEC domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the contribution by naming the released pretrained models (ARCBERT) and indicating plural models, adding detail but preserving the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement Learning enables agents to make sequential decisions to achieve specific goals in various environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement Learning enables agents to make sequential decisions to achieve specific goals in various environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in wording and meaning, so no semantic, scope, or confidence change is warranted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained module from Reinforcement Learning can be reused for similar problems, indicating its potential for character animation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained module from Reinforcement Learning can be reused for similar problems, enhancing its applicability in various domains. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the reuse claim but frames it more generally (various domains vs. specifically character animation), broadening the claimed applicability while keeping similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper surveys modern Deep Reinforcement Learning methods and their applications in Character Animation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Reinforcement Learning methods can be applied to character animation, including skeletal control and navigation for agents and virtual crowds. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's survey/contribution claim is rendered in the publication as a concrete statement about applicability to character animation; the focus on character animation remains but is presented as a conclusion rather than only a survey description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses the practical aspects of training Deep Reinforcement Learning systems and compares different frameworks for building agents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper compares different frameworks available for building Deep Reinforcement Learning agents. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core claim about comparing frameworks is preserved, but the publication expresses slightly less certainty, so confidence is tempered while the semantic content remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed concept drift detection method, ADDM, outperforms state-of-the-art drift detection methods on both synthetic and real-world data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed concept drift detection method ADDM outperforms state-of-the-art drift detection methods on both synthetic and real-world data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADDM can be integrated into any machine learning algorithm, including deep neural networks and simple linear regression models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADDM can be integrated into any machine learning algorithm, including deep neural networks and simple linear regression models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Integration capability claim is unchanged in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is theoretically guaranteed and empirically effective for detecting various concept drifts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is theoretically guaranteed and empirically effective for detecting various concept drifts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The theoretical and empirical effectiveness claim is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new method of concept drift adaptation based on the severity of the drift has been proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new method of concept drift adaptation based on the severity of the drift has been proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about severity-based adaptation is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost demonstrates to achieve the best performance overall in predicting cleared homicides at the national level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: XGBoost demonstrates to achieve the best performance overall in predicting cleared homicides at the national level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim for XGBoost is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Substantial predictive variability is detected state-wise in the prediction of cleared homicides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substantial predictive variability is detected state-wise in the prediction of cleared homicides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The observation of state-wise variability is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHAP highlights the relevance of several features in consistently predicting investigation outcomes, including homicide circumstances, weapons, victims' sex and race, and the number of involved offenders and victims. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHAP highlights the relevance of several features in consistently predicting investigation outcomes, including homicide circumstances, weapons, victims' sex and race, as well as number of involved offenders and victims. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feature-importance findings via SHAP are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable Machine Learning demonstrates to be a helpful framework for predicting homicide clearance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable Machine Learning demonstrates to be a helpful framework for predicting homicide clearance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about explainable ML as a helpful framework is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jurisdictional heterogeneity highlights the importance of developing ad hoc state-level strategies to improve police performance in clearing homicides. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Jurisdictional heterogeneity highlights the importance of developing ad hoc state-level strategies to improve police performance in clearing homicides. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The policy-relevant conclusion about jurisdictional heterogeneity is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques provide a proper alternative to solve the Boolean satisfiability problem (SAT). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques have been applied to solve the Boolean satisfiability problem (SAT). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts ML as a 'proper alternative' (strong claim) while the publication tones this down to the factual statement that ML techniques have been applied to SAT; this weakens the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolution of ML-SAT solvers ranges from naive classifiers with handcrafted features to emerging end-to-end SAT solvers like NeuroSAT. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolution of machine learning SAT solvers ranges from naive classifiers to end-to-end solvers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same evolutionary trajectory of ML-SAT solvers; differences are minor wording/details and do not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current works on solving SAT with machine learning have limitations. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Solving SAT with machine learning is identified as a promising yet challenging research topic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes 'limitations' as the broader characterization 'promising yet challenging', which clarifies the nature of limitations but slightly reduces asserted certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are possible future directions for research in solving SAT with machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining existing conflict-driven clause learning (CDCL) and local search solvers with machine learning methods has shown recent progress. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: A preprint suggestion about future directions is realized in the publication as specific observed progress (combining CDCL/local search with ML), so the speculative claim is clarified and supported by evidence (amplified).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in machine learning have surpassed human performance on several tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level statement about ML surpassing human performance does not have a counterpart in the preprint and thus represents an added contextual/result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced mathematical models of sensors for mobile robots provide an idealized characterization of task-relevant information that can be inferred from their outputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced mathematical models of touch sensors provide an idealized characterization of task-relevant information that can be inferred from their outputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'touch sensors' where the preprint used the broader term 'sensors for mobile robots', narrowing the scope to a particular sensor modality while preserving the core modeling claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models allow direct comparisons between traditional depth sensors and touch sensing, highlighting cases of interchangeability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models allow direct comparisons between traditional depth sensors and touch sensing, highlighting cases of interchangeability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparison and interchangeability claim is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models characterize unique advantages provided by touch sensing in mobile robot applications. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models characterize unique advantages provided by touch sensing over time of flight or vision sensors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparator modalities (time-of-flight or vision), clarifying which sensors touch sensing has advantages over while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results could serve as a basic building block for innovative touch sensor designs for mobile robot sensor fusion systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results could serve as a basic building block for innovative touch sensor designs for mobile robot sensor fusion systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about serving as a building block for sensor designs is identical between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Semantic Petri-Net (SPN) is proposed as a novel temporal modeling and validating method. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains no mention of SPN or any temporal modeling method; the preprint claim does not appear in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPN implements the states and transitions of the Colored Petri-Net directly based on RDFS and SPARQL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding technical implementation or Petri-net based method is present in the publication; the claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPN realizes two-way sharing of knowledge between domain semantic webs and temporal models in the runtime. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on workshop-level conclusions and does not report runtime knowledge-sharing methods; the claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several cases are provided to demonstrate the possible applications of SPN in digital twins with concurrent state changes and dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not include application cases or SPN examples; the illustrative cases claim was dropped.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The EG-ICE International Workshop on Intelligent Computing in Engineering facilitates collaboration among international experts addressing modern engineering challenges. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This workshop-level contribution is present in the publication but had no counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Many engineering tasks require open-world resolution of challenges such as multi-actor collaboration and coping with approximate models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This general engineering observation appears in the publication and was not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Feedback from addressing engineering challenges drives fundamental computer-science research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: A conclusion about feedback driving research is present in the published version but absent from the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Competence and knowledge transfer occurs in both directions between engineering and computer science. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The bidirectional knowledge-transfer conclusion is new in the publication and has no preprint match.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early detection of breast cancer in mammography screening via deep-learning based computer-aided detection systems improves curability and mortality rates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early detection of breast cancer in mammography screening via deep-learning based computer-aided detection systems shows promising potential in improving the curability and mortality rates of breast cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but softens wording to 'shows promising potential,' reducing confidence compared with the stronger preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sharing trained generative models between clinical centres can serve as a substitute for real patient data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sharing trained generative models between centres can serve as a substitute for real patient data to address data sharing restrictions due to patient privacy concerns. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit motivation (privacy/data-sharing restrictions) but preserves the core claim about using trained generative models as substitutes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to augment training datasets significantly enhances the performance of both transformer and convolutional classification models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shared Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) notably increase the performance of both transformer and convolutional classification models in mammography patch classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the task (mammography patch classification), narrowing the scope while preserving the core performance-improvement claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The exchanged intermediate results in split learning can reveal private labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exchanged intermediate results in split learning can reveal private labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding verbatim; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes cosine and Euclidean similarity measurements for gradients and smashed data, respectively. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cosine and Euclidean similarity measurements for gradients and smashed data are unified in Euclidean space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the original proposal by asserting a unification in Euclidean space, modifying the scope of the methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed label inference attacks can achieve close to 100% accuracy in recovering private labels during training and inference phases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attacks can achieve close to 100% accuracy of label attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the near-100% accuracy result, directly matching the preprint's high-accuracy claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed attack maintains accurate predictions against various state-of-the-art defense mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attacks remain effective against various state-of-the-art defense mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms that the attacks remain effective against defenses, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Three label inference attacks designed using the similarity metric can efficiently recover private labels during both training and inference phases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an explicit statement about three specific attacks and their efficiency; this specific enumeration/design detail was not present in the preprint as a standalone claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of manifold-based polynomial chaos expansion (m-PCE) and DeepONet is comparable for cases of relatively smooth input-output mappings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state that m-PCE and DeepONet are comparable for smooth mappings; that specific comparability claim is not present in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepONet shows higher accuracy when highly non-smooth dynamics is considered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepONet shows higher approximation accuracy than m-PCE when highly non-smooth dynamics is considered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the same finding about DeepONet outperforming m-PCE on non-smooth dynamics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modest over-parameterization of m-PCE leads to better generalization, while aggressive over-parameterization results in over-fitting. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modest over-parameterization of m-PCE leads to better generalization, while aggressive over-parameterization results in over-fitting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result about m-PCE over-parameterization and its effect on generalization/overfitting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A highly over-parameterized DeepONet leads to better generalization for both smooth and non-smooth dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A highly over-parameterized DeepONet leads to better generalization for both smooth and non-smooth dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding exactly; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: m-PCE can provide very good accuracy at very low training cost, whereas a highly over-parameterized DeepONet can provide better accuracy and robustness to noise but at higher training cost. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A highly over-parameterized DeepONet can provide better accuracy and robustness to noise but at a higher training cost. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the two-part tradeoff and emphasizes DeepONet's advantages with stronger certainty; the DeepONet portion is amplified and clarified in wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: m-PCE can provide very good accuracy at very low training cost. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication isolates and elevates the m-PCE low-cost accuracy statement as a standalone conclusion; this specific standalone framing was not separately enumerated in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new synthetic dataset, SynWoodScape, addresses the weaknesses of the original WoodScape dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new synthetic dataset, SynWoodScape, addresses the weaknesses of the original WoodScape dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this dataset contribution verbatim; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SynWoodScape enables the design of multi-camera algorithms to obtain a unified output in birds-eye space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SynWoodScape enables the design of multi-camera algorithms to obtain a unified output in birds-eye space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion about multi-camera algorithm design; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The release includes 80k images from the synthetic dataset with annotations for over 10 tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The release includes 80,000 images from the synthetic dataset with annotations for over 10 tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Z-Mask is also robust against possible defense-aware attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Z-Mask preserves its robustness against defense-aware attacks, making it suitable for safe and secure AI applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (higher certainty) and extends it by explicitly noting suitability for safe and secure applications, broadening the implication beyond the original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper investigates the problem of machine unlearning in Federated Learning systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper investigates the problem of machine unlearning in Federated Learning systems.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same contribution statement with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A rapid retraining approach is proposed to fully erase data samples from a trained Federated Learning model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A rapid retraining approach is proposed to fully erase data samples from a trained Federated Learning model.
alignments.rationale: The proposal is described identically in preprint and publication; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design allows data holders to jointly conduct the unlearning process efficiently while keeping their training data locally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design allows data holders to jointly conduct the unlearning process efficiently while keeping their training data locally.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication raises certainty from medium to high, indicating increased confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formal convergence and complexity analysis demonstrate that the design can preserve model utility with high efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The formal convergence and complexity analysis demonstrate that the design can preserve model utility with high efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The analytical claim is unchanged between preprint and publication in wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive evaluations on four real-world datasets illustrate the effectiveness and performance of the proposed realization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive evaluations on four real-world datasets illustrate the effectiveness and performance of the proposed realization.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation results statement is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method combines deep learning with physics-based features for improved seismic discrimination between earthquakes and explosions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enhances discrimination between earthquakes and explosions by combining physics-based features with deep learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of deep learning and physics-based features achieves better generalization performance in new regions compared to models developed solely with deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of physics-based features and deep learning achieves better generalization performance on new regions compared to models developed solely with deep learning.
alignments.rationale: The generalization result is preserved in publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visualization techniques like Grad-CAM reveal which parts of the waveform data influence deep learning decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visualizing the parts of the waveform data that dominate deep learning decisions provides insight into the decision-making processes of the model.
alignments.rationale: The visualization claim is reformulated but retains the same meaning about interpretability of model decisions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model focuses on different frequency bands for P and S waves to make its decisions.
alignments.rationale: This specific observation about frequency-band focus is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PillarGrid is a novel cooperative perception method that fuses information from multiple 3D LiDARs to enhance situation awareness for connected and automated vehicles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PillarGrid is a novel cooperative perception method that fuses information from multiple 3D LiDARs to enhance situation awareness for connected and automated vehicles.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PillarGrid consists of four main phases: cooperative preprocessing of point clouds, pillar-wise voxelization and feature extraction, grid-wise deep fusion of features from multiple sensors, and CNN-based augmented 3D object detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PillarGrid consists of four main components: cooperative preprocessing of point clouds, pillar-wise voxelization and feature extraction, grid-wise deep fusion of features from multiple sensors, and CNN-based augmented 3D object detection.
alignments.rationale: The procedural/components description remains the same, only minor wording change (phases -> components) with no semantic effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimentation shows that PillarGrid outperforms the state-of-the-art single-LiDAR-based 3D object detection methods with respect to both accuracy and range by a large margin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimentation shows that PillarGrid outperforms other single-LiDAR-based 3D object detection methods concerning both accuracy and range by a large margin.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is preserved verbatim in meaning and confidence between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based conditional inpainting method decreased the average root mean squared error (RMSE) by 52% for interpolation artifacts and 59% for double structure artifacts in in-house data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed inpainting method decreased the average root mean squared error by 52% for interpolation artifacts and 59% for double structure artifacts in the in-house data.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative RMSE improvements on in-house data are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated artifact detection achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.99 for interpolation artifacts and 0.97 for double structure artifacts using in-house data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated artifact detection revealed a ROC-AUC of 0.99 for interpolation artifacts and 0.97 for double structure artifacts using in-house data.
alignments.rationale: The automated detection AUC results are repeated verbatim and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed inpainting method removed 72% of detectable artifacts in 4D CT data with pronounced artifacts that were not part of the training set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applied to 4D CT data with pronounced artifacts not part of the training set, 72% of the detectable artifacts were removed.
alignments.rationale: The core removal rate claim is the same, but the publication presents it with higher reported certainty (publication marked as high certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results highlight the potential of deep learning-based inpainting for the restoration of artifact-affected 4D CT data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight the potential of deep learning-based inpainting for restoration of artifact-affected 4D CT data.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about potential is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: For the external test data sets, the root mean squared error improvement is similar, with reductions of 50% and 59% for interpolation and double structure artifacts, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific results on external test data that were not reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SOCKS is a data-driven stochastic optimal control toolbox based in kernel methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SOCKS is a data-driven stochastic optimal control toolbox based in kernel methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SOCKS computes approximate solutions to stochastic optimal control problems with arbitrary cost and constraint functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SOCKS computes approximate solutions to stochastic optimal control problems with arbitrary cost and constraint functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about computing approximate solutions with arbitrary costs/constraints is the same in the publication as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The toolbox is amenable to a wide variety of systems, including those with nonlinear dynamics and poorly characterized stochastic disturbances. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The toolbox is amenable to a wide variety of systems, including those with nonlinear dynamics, black-box elements, and poorly characterized stochastic disturbances. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit mention of "black-box elements," expanding the range of systems noted while otherwise preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kernel methods used in SOCKS do not place prior assumptions on the system dynamics or the structure of the uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kernel methods used in SOCKS are inherently data-driven and do not place prior assumptions on the system dynamics or the structure of the uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that the kernel methods are "inherently data-driven," but the core claim that they do not impose prior assumptions remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of trolley-style problems in the context of autonomous vehicles is a misapplication of philosophical thought experiments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of trolley-style problems in the context of autonomous vehicles is a misapplication of philosophical thought experiments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly but the content and strength of the claim remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using moral dilemmas as validation mechanisms for decision-making algorithms fails to appreciate the purpose of moral dilemmas. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of moral dilemmas in decision-making algorithms fails to appreciate the purpose of moral dilemmas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication expresses lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The misapplication of moral dilemmas in machine ethics has potentially catastrophic consequences. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The misapplication of moral dilemmas in machine ethics has potentially catastrophic consequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim but with higher expressed certainty, indicating an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are appropriate uses of moral dilemmas in machine ethics that can inform philosophical work in ethics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are appropriate uses of moral dilemmas in machine ethics that can inform philosophical work in ethics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim with similar certainty; the classification label changed but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design and evaluation activities within the TREC Legal Track from 2006 to 2011 significantly influenced current legal automation efforts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design and evaluation activities within the TREC Legal Track from 2006 to 2011 still inform legal automation efforts today. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts wording to emphasize ongoing relevance ('still inform') rather than past influence, clarifying the temporal persistence of the effect without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An interactive simulation methodology enabled collaboration between computer scientists and lawyers, allowing them to become co-designers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An interactive simulation methodology allowed computer scientists and lawyers to become co-designers, bridging the gap between computational research and real-world litigation practice. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that this co-design bridged research and practice, expanding the claim's implications; certainty is slightly lower in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The participatory approaches used in the TREC Legal Track facilitated the design and evaluation of new computational techniques for automating attorney document review. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TREC Legal Track utilized participatory approaches to facilitate the design and evaluation of new computational techniques for automating attorney document review. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim more assertively (higher certainty/type), so the core content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of the TREC Legal Track case aims to empirically support contemporary critiques of AI development and evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of the TREC Legal Track aims to empirically ground contemporary critiques of AI development and evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly ('support' vs 'ground') but the intended aim and strength remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed generalized key-value memory decouples its dimension from the number of support vectors by introducing a free parameter. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed generalized KV memory decouples its dimension from the number of support vectors by introducing a free parameter. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim in meaning and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized key-value memory provides an additional degree of freedom to control the trade-off between robustness and resource requirements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized KV memory provides an additional degree of freedom to control the tradeoff between robustness and resource requirements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved exactly in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adapting the free parameter on demand mitigates up to 44% nonidealities without the need for neural network retraining. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapting the free parameter on demand effectively mitigates up to 44% nonidealities without the need for neural network retraining. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the quantitative mitigation result and the same operational claim; wording change is minor and does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning techniques can develop fast and data-efficient jet taggers that leverage the universality of QCD. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning techniques can develop fast and data-efficient jet taggers that leverage the universality of QCD. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reliable taggers for $W$-boson and top-quark tagging can be obtained using an order of magnitude less data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reliable taggers for W-boson and top-quark tagging can be obtained using an order of magnitude less data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about data efficiency for W/top tagging is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training process can be sped up by up to a factor of three while keeping the size of the training data set fixed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training process can be sped up by up to a factor of three while keeping the size of the training data set fixed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative speedup claim with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed visual-prompt text span localizing (VPTSL) method outperforms the state-of-the-art method by 28.36% in terms of mIOU.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms several state-of-the-art methods by a large margin in terms of mIoU score.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the result that VPTSL outperforms SOTA in mIoU but omits the specific 28.36% figure, so the claim's substance is preserved but the numeric detail is removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods adopting visual span predictor perform poorly in the TAGV task due to weak correlations and huge gaps of the semantic features between the textual question and visual answer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods using a visual span-based predictor do not work well in the TAGV task due to weak correlations between the semantic features of the textual question and visual answer.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation with equivalent reasoning about weak semantic correlations; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The context query attention is utilized to perform cross-modal interaction between the extracted textual and visual features.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding contribution about 'context query attention' or explicit cross-modal interaction appears in the publication claims, so this preprint claim is not represented in the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TAGV task is formulated to predict the span of subtitles matching the visual answer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visual-prompt text span localization (VPTSL) method introduces timestamped subtitles for a text span-based predictor.
alignments.rationale: Both describe a text span-based formulation for aligning subtitles to visual answers; the publication clarifies the mechanism by mentioning timestamped subtitles for the predictor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most relevant Treasury term spread for predicting US economic recession is the 3 month to 6 month spread.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most relevant Treasury term spread for predicting US economic recession is the 3-month–6-month spread.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same specific result about the 3m–6m spread being most relevant with equivalent certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A methodology was developed for detecting relevant rules for economic recession detection using Treasury term spreads data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A methodology was developed that detected rules with high lift for predicting economic recession.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the developed methodology specifically in terms of detecting high-lift rules, which clarifies and slightly strengthens the original methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology detected rules with high lift on predicting economic recession that can be utilized by economic authorities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A methodology was developed that detected rules with high lift for predicting economic recession.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the detection of high-lift rules but drops the explicit policy/use recommendation about economic authorities, narrowing the claim to detection only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the SHAP framework aids in understanding US recession forecasts by analyzing feature importance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) framework was used to analyze feature importance in US recession forecasts.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe use of SHAP to analyze feature importance; the publication states the usage explicitly, clarifying the methodological contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the existing literature on the relevance of Treasury term spreads in predicting US economic recessions.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an interpretive conclusion placing results in context of existing literature, which was not present as an explicit claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method MetaBalance achieves a significant improvement of 8.34% in terms of NDCG@10 upon the strongest baseline on two real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method MetaBalance achieves a significant improvement of 8.34% in terms of NDCG@10 upon the strongest baseline on two real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result and dataset claims verbatim, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaBalance balances auxiliary losses by directly manipulating their gradients with respect to the shared parameters in the multi-task network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaBalance balances auxiliary losses by directly manipulating their gradients with respect to the shared parameters in the multi-task network.
alignments.rationale: The methodological description is the same in preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization imbalance problem in multi-task networks can lead to worse recommendation accuracy for the target task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimization imbalance problem in multi-task networks can lead to either auxiliary tasks dominating the network weights or being too weak to assist the target task.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more detailed characterization of how imbalance manifests (domination or weakness), clarifying the original risk to target-task accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influence of auxiliary tasks on the target task can vary dynamically throughout the training process and across different parts of the network.
alignments.rationale: No explicit publication claim restates the dynamic, varying influence across training and network parts; this nuance does not appear among the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence rate of Local SGD can be characterized as a function of the sequence of local steps during communication rounds under various settings of local functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convergence rate of Local SGD can be characterized as a function of the number of local steps during each communication round under various settings of local functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same theoretical contribution, with only minor wording differences (sequence vs number of local steps) that do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sufficient conditions on the sequence of local steps allow Local SGD to achieve linear speed-up with respect to the number of workers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sufficient conditions on the sequence of local steps allow Local SGD to achieve linear speedup with respect to the number of workers.
alignments.rationale: The result about sufficient conditions enabling linear speed-up is carried over exactly to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new communication strategy with increasing local steps is superior to existing strategies for strongly convex local functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new communication strategy with increasing local steps is superior to constant local steps for strongly convex local functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparator (constant local steps), clarifying the original broader statement about 'existing strategies.'

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For convex and nonconvex local functions, fixed local steps are the best communication strategy for Local SGD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For convex and nonconvex local functions, fixed local steps are the best communication strategy for Local SGD.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about fixed local steps being best for convex and nonconvex cases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical results are justified through extensive numerical experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical results are justified through extensive numerical experiments.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication lists this with lower reported certainty (medium vs preprint high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ALDI++ helps achieve the best forecasting error improving 6% over the winning team's approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ALDI++ helps achieve the best forecasting error improving 6% over the winning team’s approach with six times less computation time.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 6% forecasting improvement and additionally reports a computational-time benefit, broadening the claim's scope to include efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification performance improvement of ALDI++ over the original method is marginal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification performance improvement of ALDI++ over the original method is marginal.
alignments.rationale: This performance comparison is stated identically in both versions, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ALDI++ requires six times less computation time compared to the winning team's approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ALDI++ helps achieve the best forecasting error improving 6% over the winning team’s approach with six times less computation time.
alignments.rationale: The computational-time claim appears verbatim in the publication, though combined with the forecasting improvement in a single claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ALDI++ is based on previous work and bypasses user-defined parameters while taking advantage of discord similarity.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an additional methodological detail about ALDI++'s relation to prior work and parameter handling that was not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participation in ultra-distance races has increased by 1,676% in the last 23 years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participation in ultra-distance races has increased by 1,676% in the last 23 years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nearly 41% of ultra-distance runners participate in more than one race per year. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nearly 41% of ultra-distance runners participate in more than one race per year. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and reported result are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features extracted by an I3D ConvNet provide enough information to estimate the participant's performance along the different race tracks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features extracted by an I3D ConvNet provide enough information to estimate the participant’s performance along the different race tracks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried forward verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some published claims in machine learning research cannot be taken at face value due to reproducibility and replication concerns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning is facing a reproducibility and replication crisis similar to that in the social and medical sciences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the issue more strongly as a field-wide crisis and situates it relative to other sciences, expanding and strengthening the original cautionary claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an overreliance on asymptotic theory and non-credible beliefs about real-world data generating processes in both machine learning and psychology. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common themes in reform discussions include overreliance on asymptotic theory and non-credible beliefs about real-world data generating processes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes this observation as a common theme in reform discussions (slightly altering the original cross-field explicitness) but retains the core point and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Errors acknowledged in machine learning challenge the belief that optimizing predictive accuracy with large datasets eliminates the need to consider true data generating processes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are risks that arise when sources of errors in machine learning are misdiagnosed. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from challenging a specific belief about large-data optimization to a more general point about risks from misdiagnosed errors, and it expresses this with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Claims from learning in both fields are often impossible to refute due to undisclosed sources of variance in the learning pipeline. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Claims from learning in both causal attribution and predictive modeling are often implied to generalize outside the specific environment studied but are difficult to refute due to underspecification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same core point but uses the term underspecification and applies it to causal attribution and predictive modeling, clarifying scope and increasing the stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The examined 16 heatmap algorithms failed to fulfill clinical requirements to correctly indicate AI model decision process or decision quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heatmaps fail to fulfill clinical requirements to correctly indicate AI model decision processes or decision quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated in the publication with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding modality-dependent features is essential for clinical users' interpretation of AI decisions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding modality-dependent features is essential for clinical users' interpretation of AI decisions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modality-specific feature importance (MSFI) metric encodes clinical image and explanation interpretation patterns of modality prioritization and modality-specific feature localization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modality-specific feature importance (MSFI) metric encodes clinical image and explanation interpretation patterns of modality prioritization and modality-specific feature localization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried over exactly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation and MSFI metric can guide the design and selection of XAI algorithms to meet clinical requirements on multi-modal explanation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation and MSFI metric can guide the design and selection of explainable AI algorithms to meet clinical requirements on multi-modal explanation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same guidance but strengthens the statement (higher certainty) and slightly shifts phrasing to 'explainable AI', clarifying intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture combines unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), AI, and blockchain for agricultural supply-chain management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture combines unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), AI, and blockchain for agricultural supply chain management. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A fully convolutional neural network (FCN) model is utilized for biomass estimation through images captured by the UAV. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fully convolutional neural network (FCN) model is utilized for biomass estimation through images captured by UAVs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved with only minor grammatical smoothing (UAVs vs the UAV) and identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Iterative pruning is proposed to provide multiple task-specific models with various complexities and accuracy instead of a single compressed FCN model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The idea of iterative pruning is motivated to provide multiple task-specific models with various complexities and accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same motivation for iterative pruning though wording is slightly rephrased; meaning and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model selection strategy proposed will assist UAVs in updating the model based on runtime resource requirements to alleviate the impact of flight failure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model selection strategy assists UAVs in updating the model based on runtime resource requirements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about runtime-driven model updates but drops the explicit link to alleviating flight failure and increases certainty, narrowing the stated scope while strengthening confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The literature review identified relevant gaps that require further research, such as the need for dataset-independent approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The literature review identified relevant gaps that require further research, such as the need for dataset-independent approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The review conclusion is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several important factors, such as the presence of the same cars across consecutive images, have been neglected in most studies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several important factors, such as the presence of the same cars across consecutive images, have been neglected in most studies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Limitation is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of the datasets revealed that certain features, such as the availability of video sequences and images taken in diverse conditions, have not been incorporated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of the datasets revealed that certain features, such as the availability of video sequences and images taken in diverse conditions, have not been incorporated. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach utilizes a novel contrastive loss that exploits a data augmentation scheme by mixing two data samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach utilizes a novel contrastive loss that exploits a data augmentation scheme by mixing two data samples.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is identical in wording; publication increases stated certainty (medium->high), so the claim is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An unsupervised contrastive learning framework can produce high quality representations from time series data.
alignments.rationale: This general, higher-level statement about producing high-quality representations does not have a direct counterpart in the preprint and appears as an added, more general claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PROSUB outperforms the winner of the MUDI MICCAI challenge, producing large improvements greater than 18% MSE on the MUDI challenge sub-tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PROSUB outperforms the winner of the MUDI MICCAI challenge, producing large improvements greater than 18% MSE on the MUDI challenge sub-tasks.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication with no change in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PROSUB integrates a neural architecture search (NAS) paradigm, allowing the network architecture hyperparameters to respond to the subsampling process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PROSUB integrates a neural architecture search (NAS) paradigm, allowing the network architecture hyperparameters to respond to the subsampling process.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is word-for-word the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PROSUB improves optimization stability by subsampling with a hard decision boundary and using recursive feature elimination during deep learning training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PROSUB improves optimization stability by subsampling with a hard decision boundary during deep learning training.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the recursive feature elimination component, so the claim is revised and narrowed to only the hard decision boundary subsampling mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generalizes to other problems beyond MRI measurement selection-reconstruction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology of PROSUB generalizes beyond MRI measurement selection-reconstruction to problems that subsample and reconstruct multi-channeled data.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the kinds of problems the method generalizes to (subsample+reconstruct multi-channeled data), which narrows and clarifies the original broader generalization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general procedure is presented to quantify the performance of NN-based controllers in model predictive control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general procedure is presented to quantify the performance of NN-based controllers in model predictive control.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Suitable conditions involving the worst-case error and the Lipschitz constant are established to guarantee the stability of the closed-loop system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Suitable conditions involving the worst-case error and the Lipschitz constant are established to guarantee the stability of the closed-loop system.
alignments.rationale: The stability conditions claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An offline, mixed-integer optimization-based method is developed to compute the worst-case error and the Lipschitz constant exactly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An offline, mixed-integer optimization-based method is developed to compute the worst-case error and the Lipschitz constant exactly.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is expressed the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The techniques provided are sufficient to certify the stability and performance of a ReLU-based approximation of an MPC control law.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The techniques provided are sufficient to certify the stability and performance of an ReLU-based approximation of an MPC control law.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a PID controller as the trainable RL policy simplifies the implementation of deep reinforcement learning in control systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a PID controller as the trainable RL policy simplifies the implementation of deep RL in control systems.
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing a PID-as-policy approach is unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PID controller approach does not require additional hardware, as it can be implemented through a standard programmable logic controller.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PID controller approach does not require additional hardware, as it can be implemented through a standard programmable logic controller.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The final product of the approach is a well-tuned PID controller that practitioners can reason about and deploy with confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method introduces novel reward functions that encourage the generation of candidate summaries satisfying both lexical and semantic criteria.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extremely low resolution quantized synapses with large stochastic variations can achieve high testing accuracies compared to Deep Neural Networks using floating precision weights.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Voltage controlled Domain Wall devices demonstrate stochastic behavior but can be extremely energy efficient during both training and inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modifications to learning algorithms can address the stochastic behavior and mitigate the effects of low-resolution to achieve high testing accuracies.
